title: Write Unit and Integration Tests for Landing Page
description: Ensure the landing page is covered by automated tests for rendering, navigation, and accessibility.
details: Using React Testing Library and Jest (or the project's test stack), write tests to cover: correct rendering of all major elements, navigation links, and accessibility (e.g., ARIA roles, keyboard navigation). Ensure tests are placed in the appropriate __tests__ or *.test.tsx files.
test_strategy: Run all tests and confirm 100% coverage for the landing page component and its routing. Use coverage reports to verify. Fix any failing or flaky tests.

title: Create Landing Page Component
description: Develop a new React component for the landing page in the frontend application.
details: Create a new file (e.g., LandingPage.tsx) in the appropriate directory under apps/frontend/src/pages or similar. The component should include a welcoming headline, a brief project description, and clear navigation links to other main sections (e.g., About, Docs, API Demo). Use functional components and TypeScript. Apply existing design system or base styles if present.
test_strategy: Render the component in isolation and verify that all expected elements (headline, description, navigation links) are present. Use React Testing Library for unit tests to check for correct rendering and accessibility (e.g., headings, links).

title: Integrate Landing Page with Routing
description: Add the landing page component to the React Router configuration as the root (/) route.
details: Update the main router file (e.g., App.tsx or routes.tsx) to set the new LandingPage component as the default route for '/'. Ensure that navigation to '/' renders the landing page and that other routes remain unaffected. If a placeholder or sample homepage exists, replace it with the new landing page.
test_strategy: Manually navigate to '/' in the running app and verify the landing page displays. Add integration tests to confirm correct routing and that navigation links work as expected.

title: Add Basic Styling and Responsive Layout
description: Apply styling to the landing page to ensure it is visually appealing and responsive across devices.
details: Use existing CSS modules, styled-components, or the project's preferred styling approach. Ensure the landing page looks good on desktop and mobile screens. Follow any existing design tokens or style guides. Include at least a hero section, call-to-action, and navigation area.
test_strategy: Test visually in browser at multiple screen sizes. Use unit or snapshot tests to verify classNames/styles are applied. Optionally, use accessibility tools (axe, Lighthouse) to check for contrast and layout issues.
